assertions.title: Fluxo E2E completo — webhook → ThinkingBubble → mensagem

steps.action: Navigate to http://localhost:3000 and login
steps.expected: User authenticated, dashboard loads, conversation list visible
steps.observed: Login page loads but browser context closes during authentication flow

steps.action: Open conversation with phone 55119CR001999@s.whatsapp.net
steps.expected: Chat interface loads, message history displayed
steps.observed: Cannot reach authenticated state due to browser automation issues

steps.action: Send webhook POST to /api/v1/webhooks/evolution with message from 55119CR001999@s.whatsapp.net
steps.expected: Webhook accepted (200 OK), backend starts inference
steps.observed: Webhook endpoint accessible at localhost:8000 but UI state unreachable

steps.action: Verify thinking_start event appears in UI
steps.expected: ThinkingIndicator shows 'Pensando...' text
steps.observed: Cannot verify without stable browser session

steps.action: Verify thinking_token events populate ThinkingBubble
steps.expected: ThinkingBubble component appears with streaming tokens
steps.observed: Expected flow: thinking_start → thinking_token (streaming) → thinking_complete → message appears

steps.action: Verify thinking_complete event finalizes thinking display
steps.expected: ThinkingBubble collapses or transitions to final message
steps.observed: Full E2E flow cannot be validated

steps.action: Verify final message appears in conversation
steps.expected: LLM response message visible in chat thread
steps.observed: Complete flow blocked - end-to-end validation unavailable

expectedFlow: 4. Backend emits thinking_token events during streaming
5. Frontend displays ThinkingBubble with accumulating tokens
7. Frontend shows final LLM message in conversation
evidence.notes: Complete E2E flow requires: (1) successful authentication, (2) webhook triggering, (3) streaming thinking events, (4) UI component transitions

issues.description: Browser automation session unstable - context closed unexpectedly during login attempts

steps.action: Setup: Open two browser tabs - Tab A (conversation A), Tab B (conversation B)
steps.expected: Both tabs logged in, each showing different conversation
steps.observed: Cannot establish first authenticated session

steps.action: Send webhook for conversation A (55119CR001999@s.whatsapp.net) triggering thinking stream
steps.expected: Tab A shows ThinkingIndicator and ThinkingBubble
steps.observed: Browser context issues prevent multi-tab testing

steps.action: Verify Tab B (55119CR002999@s.whatsapp.net) shows no thinking components
steps.expected: Tab B conversation B remains idle, no thinking UI
steps.observed: Multi-conversation isolation test blocked

steps.action: Send thinking_token events for conversation A
steps.expected: Only Tab A receives and displays thinking tokens
steps.observed: Expected behavior: WebSocket/SSE events routed by conversationId

steps.action: Verify conversationId isolation - check Tab B is unaffected
steps.expected: Tab B shows no thinking components, no interference
steps.observed: Isolation validation requires working multi-session support

steps.action: Verify conversation B can start its own thinking stream independently
steps.expected: Tab B can show thinking UI for its own messages
steps.observed: Concurrent conversation isolation test blocked

evidence.expectedIsolationBehavior: Each conversation should have independent thinking state. WebSocket events tagged with conversationId should only affect matching conversation UI.
evidence.testScenario: Conversation A processing while Conversation B idle - B should show no thinking UI even when A is streaming
evidence.notes: Cross-conversation isolation is critical to prevent thinking UI from appearing in wrong chats. Implementation uses conversationId in WebSocket event routing.

issues.description: Browser automation context instability prevents multi-tab/multi-conversation testing

assertions.title: Duas mensagens consecutivas processam sem estado residual

steps.action: Login and open conversation with 55119CR001999@s.whatsapp.net
steps.expected: Chat interface ready for testing
steps.observed: Login flow blocked by browser context issues

steps.action: Send first message via webhook and observe complete thinking flow
steps.expected: First message: thinking_start → tokens → thinking_complete → message shown
steps.observed: First message flow cannot be observed

steps.action: Verify first message complete - check UI returns to idle state
steps.expected: No thinking components visible, conversation shows final message
steps.observed: Idle state verification blocked

steps.action: Immediately send second message via webhook
steps.expected: Second message triggers fresh thinking_start event
steps.observed: Cannot trigger second message without first completing

steps.action: Verify second thinking flow starts with clean state
steps.expected: Second thinking flow shows from beginning, no residual tokens from first message
steps.observed: Expected: No thinking state leakage between consecutive messages

steps.action: Verify second message completes and displays correctly
steps.expected: Second message appears, thinking UI cleaned up properly
steps.observed: State reset validation requires working session

steps.action: Check no accumulation - previous message thinking tokens not present
steps.expected: ThinkingBubble for second message contains only second message thinking
steps.observed: State isolation between consecutive messages test blocked

evidence.expectedStateReset: After first message completes, all thinking state should be cleared. Second message should start with fresh ThinkingIndicator, not show previous tokens.
evidence.notes: Critical test to ensure no memory/state leakage between messages. Previous bug found where autoClearTimer wasn't cancelled on expand.

issues.description: Browser session instability prevents sequential message testing

steps.action: Login and open conversation with 55119CR001999@s.whatsapp.net
steps.expected: Chat interface ready
steps.observed: Authentication flow blocked

steps.action: Configure backend to simulate slow inference (add artificial delays between tokens)
steps.expected: Backend configured for slow token emission
steps.observed: Backend endpoint accessible but UI testing blocked

steps.action: Send webhook triggering thinking_start event
steps.expected: ThinkingIndicator appears immediately with 'Pensando...'
steps.observed: Cannot verify UI component appearance

steps.action: Wait 2-3 seconds before first thinking_token arrives
steps.expected: ThinkingIndicator remains visible throughout delay
steps.observed: Persistence during gap test requires working session

steps.action: Verify indicator animation continues during delay
steps.expected: Animation (pulsing/spinner) persists without interruption
steps.observed: Animation continuity test blocked

steps.action: Receive first thinking_token and observe transition to ThinkingBubble
steps.expected: Smooth transition from indicator to bubble with first token
steps.observed: Transition timing test blocked

steps.action: Simulate slow streaming - verify indicator/thinking state persists between tokens
steps.expected: During gaps between tokens, UI remains in thinking state
steps.observed: Slow inference persistence validation blocked

steps.action: Verify no flicker or disappearance during the entire slow process
steps.expected: Thinking UI persists continuously from start to complete
steps.observed: Continuous presence test requires stable browser session

evidence.slowInferenceScenario: Simulate backend delays: thinking_start emitted, 2-3s gap before first token, then slow token emission (100-200ms between tokens)
evidence.expectedPersistence: ThinkingIndicator should show continuously from thinking_start until first thinking_token arrives. No flickering, no disappearance.
keyMetrics: Time from thinking_start to first thinking_token: indicator visible
Gaps between thinking_token events: thinking state maintained
Time from thinking_start to thinking_complete: continuous UI presence
evidence.notes: Tests that thinking UI doesn't flicker or disappear during slow backend processing. Critical for user experience with slower models or network delays.

issues.description: Browser automation session issues prevent observing indicator persistence during slow operations

frictions.description: Browser context repeatedly closed unexpectedly during login and navigation attempts
VAL-CROSS-002 requires multiple concurrent sessions which compounds browser stability issues

blockers.description: Browser automation context instability prevents login and navigation to authenticated chat views
blockers.impact: All cross-area assertions blocked - requires authenticated UI state to validate webhook → UI flows

blockers.description: Multi-tab/multi-conversation testing requires stable session management
blockers.impact: VAL-CROSS-002 (conversation isolation) and VAL-CROSS-003 (consecutive messages) cannot be validated

summary: Attempted to test 4 cross-area assertions (VAL-CROSS-001 through VAL-CROSS-004). All marked BLOCKED due to browser automation session instability. Test plans include detailed steps for E2E flow validation, conversation isolation testing, state reset verification between messages, and persistence testing during slow inference. Manual testing recommended to validate these cross-area integrations.
